learningObjectives: Use core food and shopping vocabulary at A2 level.
Use quantifiers much, many, a few and a little correctly.
Use comparatives, superlatives, less and fewer when shopping.
Complete a short text about shopping with grammatical control.
Understand transactional exchanges at shops and markets.
Write a short message about a shopping experience.
Answer interview questions about food and shopping habits.
grammarFocus: Quantifiers: much, many, a few, a little, all, every

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: Keep your receipt if you want a refund.
There is a ten-percent discount on fruit today.
The shop assistant helped the customer.
I placed an order for two pizzas.
Salt is an important ingredient in this soup.
She asked for a refund because the bread was old.
These shoes were a real bargain.
We paid at the checkout near the exit.
We buy most food at the supermarket.
There was a long queue at the checkout.
He paid from his wallet at the counter.
Look at the menu before you order.

grammarReference.structure: Quantifiers: much, many, a few, a little, all, every
grammarReference.explanation: Use many with countable nouns and much with uncountable nouns, usually in questions and negatives. Use a few (countable) and a little (uncountable) for small positive amounts. Use all and every to talk about complete groups.
examples: How many apples do we need?
I bought a few eggs and a little milk.
Every customer gets a discount today.
commonMistakes: How much apples? (×) → How many apples? (✓)
I have a few milk (×) → I have a little milk (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use comparatives (-er or more) to compare two things. Use superlatives (-est or most) for the extreme in a group. Use fewer with countable nouns and less with uncountable nouns.
examples: This shop is cheaper than the market.
It is the best bargain in the store.
We need less salt in this recipe.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Food and Shopping Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve food and shopping words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Shopping Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: The paper that shows what you paid is a _____.
questions.explanation: Receipt (hóa đơn) chứng minh thanh toán. Refund là hoàn tiền.

questions.questionText: When the price is lower than usual, there is a _____.
questions.explanation: Discount (giảm giá) làm giá rẻ hơn.

questions.questionText: A person who buys things in a shop is a _____.
questions.explanation: Customer (khách hàng) là người mua hàng.

questions.questionText: Flour, eggs and milk are _____ in a cake recipe.
questions.explanation: Ingredient (nguyên liệu) là thành phần nấu ăn.

questions.questionText: If the shop gives your money back, you get a _____.
questions.explanation: Refund (hoàn tiền) khi trả hàng hoặc hàng lỗi.

questions.questionText: Something very cheap and good value is a _____.
questions.explanation: Bargain (món hời) là mua được giá tốt.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the shopping words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với nghĩa rõ ràng. Order và checkout thường bị nhầm.

pairs.right: a request to buy food or goods
the place where you pay in a shop
a list of food you can choose in a café

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: We paid at the _____ near the supermarket exit.
questions.explanation: Checkout (quầy thanh toán) là nơi trả tiền.

questions.questionText: She kept the _____ in her _____ after paying.
questions.explanation: Receipt (hóa đơn) thường cất trong wallet (ví).

questions.questionText: Many _____ were waiting because the _____ was very long.
questions.explanation: Customers (khách hàng) xếp queue (hàng đợi).

questions.questionText: He found a great _____ on shoes at the _____.
questions.explanation: Bargain (món hời) tại supermarket (siêu thị).

exercises.title: Apply: Words in Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: Keep your [0] for a [1]. | There is a [2] on rice today.
questions.explanation: receipt (hóa đơn); refund (hoàn tiền); discount (giảm giá).

content.template: Keep your [0] for a [1]. There is a [2] on rice today.

questions.questionText: Complete: Check the [0] before you [1]. | We joined the [2] at the checkout.
questions.explanation: menu (thực đơn); order (gọi món); queue (hàng đợi).

content.template: Check the [0] before you [1]. We joined the [2] at the checkout.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Quantifiers and Comparisons
lessons.learningObjective: Use quantifiers and comparatives/superlatives when talking about shopping.

exercises.title: Learn: Much, Many, Fewer
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: How _____ apples do we need for the recipe?
questions.explanation: Apples đếm được → many.

questions.questionText: There isn't _____ sugar in the bowl.
questions.explanation: Sugar không đếm được → much.

questions.questionText: I bought _____ eggs and _____ milk.
questions.explanation: a few + countable; a little + uncountable.

questions.questionText: This market is _____ than the supermarket near my house.
questions.explanation: So sánh hơn với cheap → cheaper.

questions.questionText: I eat _____ snacks now because I want to be healthier.
questions.explanation: Snacks đếm được → fewer.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: How [0] customers are in the queue? | We need [1] salt. | [2] item is on discount today.
questions.explanation: many (customers); little (salt); Every (item).

content.template: How [0] customers are in the queue? We need [1] salt. [2] item is on discount today.

questions.questionText: Complete: This is the [0] bargain in the shop. | I spend [1] money on sweets. | There are [2] ingredients in the basket.
questions.explanation: best (superlative); less (money); a few (ingredients).

content.template: This is the [0] bargain in the shop. I spend [1] money on sweets. There are [2] ingredients in the basket.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: _____ customer must show a receipt for a refund.
questions.explanation: Every + singular noun: Every customer.

choices.distractorNote: All customers needs plural verb pattern

questions.questionText: GreenMart has _____ vegetables than the small shop on our street.
questions.explanation: Comparative of many → more.

questions.questionText: We bought only _____ rice because we don't need _____.
questions.explanation: a little + uncountable rice; much in negative.

questions.questionText: The checkout queue today is _____ than yesterday.
questions.explanation: Long → comparative longer.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: There / are / a few / customers / at / the checkout.
questions.explanation: There are a few customers at the checkout. — a few + countable.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: This / supermarket / is / cheaper / than / the market.
questions.explanation: This supermarket is cheaper than the market. — comparative.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: I / need / a little / sugar / for / the recipe.
questions.explanation: I need a little sugar for the recipe. — a little + uncountable.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Shopping Article
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short shopping text and complete meaning with grammatical control.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Smart Shopping at GreenMart
passage.text:
Last Saturday, Linh went to GreenMart supermarket with her mother. They wanted to buy ingredients for a family dinner, but they also hoped to find a bargain.

First, they checked the discount labels near the checkout. Many customers were pushing trolleys and reading price tags carefully. Linh's mother compared two brands of rice and chose the cheaper one. "We don't need much sugar," she said, "just a little for the recipe."

At the meat counter, Linh asked the assistant about a refund policy. The assistant explained that customers could return items with a receipt within seven days. Linh felt happier because she had forgotten an ingredient last week and lost money.

Before they paid, they joined a short queue at the checkout. The total was lower than Linh expected because of a ten-percent discount on vegetables. On the way home, Linh decided she would shop more carefully every week. "Fewer impulse buys mean more savings," her mother smiled.
passage.imagePrompt: A bright supermarket with discount signs, customers at checkout queues, and a shopping trolley full of groceries.

questions.questionText: Why did Linh and her mother go to GreenMart?
questions.explanation: Buy ingredients for a family dinner.

choices.text: To eat at the café menu

questions.questionText: How long can customers return items with a receipt?
questions.explanation: Return items with a receipt within seven days.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Smart Shopping at GreenMart
passage.text:

First, they checked the discount labels near the checkout. Many customers were pushing trolleys and reading price tags carefully. Linh's mother compared two brands of rice and chose the cheaper one. "We don't need much sugar," she said, "just a little for the recipe."

At the meat counter, Linh asked the assistant about a refund policy. The assistant explained that customers could return items with a receipt within seven days. Linh felt happier because she had forgotten an ingredient last week and lost money.

Before they paid, they joined a short queue at the checkout. The total was lower than Linh expected because of a ten-percent discount on vegetables. On the way home, Linh decided she would shop more carefully every week. "Fewer impulse buys mean more savings," her mother smiled.
passage.imagePrompt: A bright supermarket with discount signs, customers at checkout queues, and a shopping trolley full of groceries.

questions.questionText: In the article, 'a bargain' means _____.
questions.explanation: Hoped to find a bargain — món hời, giá tốt.

choices.text: something bought at a good price

questions.questionText: Why was the total lower than Linh expected?
questions.explanation: Ten-percent discount on vegetables.

choices.text: Because of a discount on vegetables

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the article one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: Smart Shopping at GreenMart
passage.text:

First, they checked the discount labels near the checkout. Many customers were pushing trolleys and reading price tags carefully. Linh's mother compared two brands of rice and chose the cheaper one. "We don't need much sugar," she said, "just a little for the recipe."

passage.imagePrompt: A bright supermarket with discount signs, customers at checkout queues, and a shopping trolley full of groceries.

questions.questionText: What is the main message of the article?
questions.explanation: Bài kể chuyến mua sắm thông minh và tiết kiệm.

choices.text: Careful shopping can help you save money

questions.questionText: Put the events in order: (1) Paid at checkout (2) Compared prices (3) Asked about refund policy
questions.explanation: So sánh giá → hỏi refund → thanh toán.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Article
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

passage.imagePrompt: A bright supermarket with discount signs, customers at checkout queues, and a shopping trolley full of groceries.

questions.questionText: Match each item from the article to the correct fact.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với chi tiết trong bài.

pairs.right: Needed for a refund within seven days

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Shopping Conversations
lessons.learningObjective: Understand main ideas and details in transactional shopping exchanges.

exercises.title: Learn: At the Checkout
exercises.instructions: Listen to the conversation. Answer the questions.

script.title: At the Supermarket Checkout

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Hello. Did you find everything today?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes, thanks. I'd like to order these ingredients for soup, please.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: That's twelve pounds forty. There's a discount on tomatoes today.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Great. Can I have a receipt, please? I might need a refund if the milk is bad.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Of course. Keep the receipt. Next customer, please!

script.audioNotes: Busy supermarket sounds in background, clear friendly voices. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: What is Linh buying?
questions.explanation: Order these ingredients for soup.

questions.questionText: How much does Linh pay?
questions.explanation: That's twelve pounds forty.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Checkout
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same conversation. Answer the next questions.

script.title: At the Supermarket Checkout

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Hello. Did you find everything today?

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes, thanks. I'd like to order these ingredients for soup, please.

lines.speaker: Assistant

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Great. Can I have a receipt, please? I might need a refund if the milk is bad.

lines.speaker: Assistant
lines.text: Of course. Keep the receipt. Next customer, please!

script.audioNotes: Busy supermarket sounds in background, clear friendly voices. Approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: Which item has a discount today?
questions.explanation: There's a discount on tomatoes today.

questions.questionText: Why does Linh want a receipt?
questions.explanation: Might need a refund if the milk is bad.

choices.text: In case she needs a refund

exercises.title: Check: Market Bargain
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Weekend Market Bargain

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Good morning! Fresh fruit today. Three mangoes for two pounds — a real bargain!

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: That's cheaper than the supermarket. I'll take six, please.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Many customers buy extra for smoothies. Do you need a bag?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Yes, please. Here's four pounds. Keep the change.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Thank you! Enjoy your shopping.

script.audioNotes: Outdoor market ambience, clear prices and numbers. Approx. 38 seconds.

questions.questionText: What bargain does the vendor offer first?
questions.explanation: Three mangoes for two pounds.

questions.questionText: How many mangoes does Minh buy and how much does he pay?
questions.explanation: I'll take six... Here's four pounds.

questions.questionText: Why does Minh buy at the market?
questions.explanation: That's cheaper than the supermarket.

choices.text: Because it is cheaper than the supermarket

exercises.title: Apply: Match What You Heard
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the checkout conversation. Match each item to the correct detail.

questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với chi tiết trong hội thoại.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Message About Shopping
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message about a shopping trip using unit vocabulary and quantifiers.

exercises.title: Learn: Shopping Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: receipt, discount, bargain, checkout, ingredients.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi Mum, | I found a [0] on fruit. | I paid at the [1]. | Here is the [2].
questions.explanation: bargain (món hời); checkout (quầy thanh toán); receipt (hóa đơn).

content.template:
Hi Mum,

I found a [0] on fruit. I paid at the [1]. Here is the [2].

exercises.title: Practice: Build Shopping Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: There / are / many / customers / in / the queue.
questions.explanation: There are many customers in the queue. — many + countable.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: This / shop / is / cheaper / than / the supermarket.
questions.explanation: This shop is cheaper than the supermarket. — comparative.

exercises.title: Check: Message About Your Shopping
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a friend. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: You went shopping at the supermarket yesterday. Write a short message to your friend Anna about your experience.
prompts: Say where you went and what you bought (ingredients or food).
Mention if there was a discount or a bargain.
Say something about the queue or checkout.
End the message in a friendly way.
successCriteria: Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Anna,

I went to GreenMart yesterday and bought ingredients for dinner. There was a discount on vegetables, so it was a real bargain! Many customers were in the queue, but the checkout was fast. I kept my receipt in case I need a refund.

See you,
Linh

grammar.criteria: Uses quantifiers or comparatives (many, a few, cheaper) correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses shopping words (discount, receipt, checkout, bargain, ingredient) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, details and a friendly closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Describes a shopping trip with discount or bargain; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Tom, | I bought [0] eggs and [1] milk. | The shop was [2] than last week. | Bye, | Mai
questions.explanation: a few (eggs); a little (milk); cheaper (comparative).

content.template:
Dear Tom,

I bought [0] eggs and [1] milk. The shop was [2] than last week.

Bye,
Mai

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Shopping
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about food and shopping using unit vocabulary and grammar.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Where do you usually buy food? You say:
questions.explanation: Trả lời tự nhiên về nơi mua sắm.

choices.text: I usually receipt food at home.
choices.distractorNote: Receipt is not a verb here

choices.text: I usually buy food at the supermarket near my home.

choices.distractorNote: Queue is not a verb here

questions.questionText: Examiner: Do you look for discounts? You say:
questions.explanation: Yes + discount — thói quen mua sắm.

choices.text: Yes, I always check refund labels before I buy.
Yes, I always check discount labels before I buy.

questions.questionText: Examiner: How much fruit do you eat? You say:
questions.explanation: Much không dùng khẳng định → a lot of / quite a lot.

choices.text: I eat much fruit every week.
I eat quite a lot of fruit every week.
I eat many fruit every week.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Tell me about a bargain you found. You say:
questions.explanation: Bargain + giá rẻ — cấu trúc kể chuyện A2.

choices.text: Last month I found a receipt — shoes were half price.
Last month I found a bargain — shoes were half price with a discount.
Last month I found a queue — shoes were half price.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Is the market cheaper than the supermarket? You say:
questions.explanation: Comparative: cheaper than.

choices.text: Yes, the market is cheap than the supermarket.
Yes, the market is usually cheaper than the supermarket.
Yes, the market is more cheaper than the supermarket.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What do you do at the checkout? You say:
questions.explanation: Mô tả hành động tại quầy thanh toán.

choices.text: I pay and ask for a menu.
I pay and ask for a receipt.
I pay and ask for an ingredient.

exercises.title: Check: Shopping Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about food and shopping. Speak for up to two minutes.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each shopping situation.

questions.questionText: The milk you bought is bad. At the shop you say:
questions.explanation: Yêu cầu refund với receipt — tình huống thực tế.

choices.text: Excuse me, can I have a discount? Here is my menu.
Excuse me, can I have a refund? Here is my receipt.
Excuse me, can I have a queue? Here is my bargain.

questions.questionText: A friend asks if the queue is long. You say:
questions.explanation: Mô tả queue với many customers.

choices.text: Yes, there is much customers waiting at the checkout.
Yes, there are many customers waiting at the checkout.
Yes, there are much customer waiting at the checkout.

questions.questionText: You are ordering in a café. You say:
questions.explanation: Look at menu before order — cụm tự nhiên.

choices.text: Can I see the receipt, please? I'd like to order soup.
Can I see the menu, please? I'd like to order soup.
Can I see the refund, please? I'd like to order soup.
